question.text: A cotton merchant enters forward sale at Rs 18,000/bale for April delivery. He buys a March 31 call option at strike Rs 17,500 with premium Rs 500. If cotton spot drops to Rs 16,000 on March 31, what is his net gain per bale?

answer.text: Rs 1,500 (forward sale revenue minus spot cost minus unexercised option premium)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Forward sale price = 18,000. Spot purchase = 16,000. Call not exercised (spot < strike). Premium lost = 500. Net gain = 18,000 - 16,000 - 500 = Rs 1,500/bale. **The Trap:** Rs 2,000 ignores premium. Rs 500 is just the premium. Rs 1,000 miscalculates. The call option was insurance — not needed since prices fell, but premium is still a cost. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers option hedging P&L with forward commitments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Had cotton risen to Rs 19,000, the merchant would exercise the call (buy at 17,500) and deliver against the forward sale at 18,000 — the option protected against the upside risk.
